name: Google Analytics
description: Tells Google how long you spent on this page, what you clicked, and where you came from.

name: Google Tag Manager
description: A hub that loads other tracking scripts onto the page on Google's behalf.

name: Google Ads
description: Tracks whether you clicked a Google ad and what you did after — used to bill advertisers and target you.

name: Facebook Pixel
description: Reports your actions on this site back to Facebook so it can target you with ads and track ad conversions.

name: Instagram / Meta
description: Loads Meta social features and can track your browsing even if you're not on Instagram.

name: Twitter / X Pixel
description: Tells X/Twitter what pages you visit so it can link your browsing to your Twitter account for ad targeting.

name: LinkedIn Insight Tag
description: Tracks your visits to build a professional profile of your browsing habits for LinkedIn ad targeting.

name: TikTok Pixel
description: Reports your activity on this site to TikTok for ad targeting and conversion tracking.

name: Pinterest Tag
description: Tracks what you do after seeing a Pinterest ad, and helps Pinterest target you with more ads.

name: Snapchat Pixel
description: Connects your actions on this site to your Snapchat account for ad targeting.

name: Reddit Pixel
description: Tells Reddit what you did on this site so it can link your browsing to your Reddit account.

name: Hotjar
description: Records your mouse movements, scrolling, and clicks — essentially a video of your session on this site.

name: Microsoft Clarity
description: Microsoft's session recording tool — watches how you navigate the page including heatmaps and replays.

name: Bing Ads / Microsoft Ads
description: Tracks conversions from Bing/Microsoft ads and helps Microsoft target you with ads across the web.

name: Mixpanel
description: Tracks detailed events (button clicks, form submissions, page views) to analyze how you use the product.

name: Amplitude
description: Logs detailed product analytics — which features you use, how often, and in what order.

name: Segment
description: A data pipeline that collects your behavior and sends it to dozens of other analytics and ad platforms.

name: Heap
description: Automatically records every click, tap, and page view without the site needing to tag individual events.

name: FullStory
description: Records complete session replays of your visit — every mouse move, scroll, and click.

name: LogRocket
description: Records session replays and performance data — a detailed log of everything you did on the page.

name: Mouseflow
description: Records your mouse movements and creates heatmaps showing where users look and click.

name: Crazy Egg
description: Tracks clicks and scrolling to generate heatmaps of how users interact with the page.

name: Optimizely
description: Runs A/B tests by showing different versions of the page to different users — and tracks your behavior in each.

name: VWO (Visual Website Optimizer)
description: Runs A/B tests and records how different users interact with page variations.

name: Criteo
description: A retargeting ad network — if you looked at a product, Criteo shows you that product as an ad across other websites.

name: Outbrain
description: Powers the 'You may also like' sponsored content links at the bottom of articles — and tracks which ones you click.

name: Taboola
description: Serves sponsored 'recommended content' widgets and tracks your engagement to target you elsewhere.

name: Amazon Ads
description: Tracks your browsing so Amazon can show you relevant ads both on and off Amazon.

name: Quantcast
description: Collects data about your demographics and interests to help advertisers target audiences like you.

name: Intercom
description: Powers the live chat widget — also tracks your activity on the site to personalize support conversations.

name: Zendesk
description: Customer support chat and ticketing — tracks your interactions to manage support history.

name: Drift
description: A chatbot and live chat tool that also identifies you as a sales lead and tracks your site activity.

name: HubSpot
description: Tracks your visits and form submissions to build a sales/marketing profile about you in their CRM.

name: Salesforce / Pardot
description: Marketing automation that links your browsing behavior to your email address if you've ever filled out a form.

name: Marketo
description: Marketing automation that tracks your visits and scores you as a potential customer.

name: AddThis
description: Social sharing buttons that also track your browsing across every site that uses them.

name: ShareThis
description: Social sharing widget that tracks which articles you read and share.

name: Disqus
description: Comment system that tracks your reading and commenting behavior across all sites using Disqus.

name: Yandex Metrica
description: Russian analytics platform that records session replays and tracks visitor behavior.

name: New Relic
description: Performance monitoring that collects browser timing data — less about you, more about the site's speed.

name: Datadog RUM
description: Real user monitoring that logs page load times and errors from your browser session.

name: Sentry
description: Error tracking that captures JavaScript errors and may include a breadcrumb trail of your recent actions.

name: Cloudflare Insights
description: Cloudflare's analytics that measures page load speed and visitor counts — minimal personal data.

name: Braze
description: Customer engagement platform that tracks your behavior to power personalized push notifications and emails.

name: Klaviyo
description: Email marketing tracker — links your site visits to your email address for personalized campaign targeting.

name: Chartbeat
description: Real-time analytics for news sites — tracks which articles you read and for how long.

name: Comscore
description: Audience measurement company — aggregates your browsing data to sell demographic reports to media companies.

name: Nielsen
description: Collects audience data to measure reach and sell market research — your visit contributes to Nielsen ratings.

name: LiveRamp
description: Data broker that links your browsing to your real identity and sells that linkage to advertisers.

name: Twilio SendGrid
description: Email delivery service — the tracking pixel in emails tells the sender when you opened them.
